title: Ipc Handler Registry
description: Electron desktop host concern â€” **Ipc Handler Registry**. Local mode uses an SDK-bootstrapped kernel in the main process and exposes IPC-backed ports; remote mode reuses the HTTP client stack. Register main-process IPC channels that implement `client:port-*` semantics without HTTP.
context: Electron desktop host concern â€” **Ipc Handler Registry**. Local mode uses an SDK-bootstrapped kernel in the main process and exposes IPC-backed ports; remote mode reuses the HTTP client stack. Register main-process IPC channels that implement `client:port-*` semantics without HTTP.

rules:
Each handler MUST call the same kernel use cases as the HTTP API would for the equivalent port method (no duplicated business rules in the renderer). Kernel access MUST go through the process-scoped SDK host context created by `createSdkContext`.

v1 local desktop MUST implement draft-aware port methods `previewChangeDraft`, `outlineChangeArtifact`, and `outlineSpecDraft` via the same kernel use cases as `POST` preview/outline routes.

rules.requirement: SDK kernel access in IPC handlers
rules: IPC handlers MUST invoke kernel use cases on the process-scoped kernel from `createSdkContext`. They MUST NOT construct a separate kernel per handler invocation.

rules.requirement: graph IPC methods use the Electron graph runtime
rules:
Desktop-local graph IPC methods MUST create and use their graph provider from
`@specd/code-graph-electron`.

The Electron main process MUST keep graph execution inside the desktop-local host
runtime, including the SQLite-backed local graph operations exposed through IPC.
Renderer code MUST continue to call the shared `SpecdDataPort` surface and MUST
NOT import graph runtime packages directly.

scenarios.name: Renderer does not embed business rules
then: no duplicated transition logic in preload

scenarios.requirement: SDK kernel access in IPC handlers
scenarios.name: IPC handler uses SDK-bootstrapped kernel
given: a local desktop project is open
when: an IPC handler executes a SpecdDataPort operation
then: it invokes use cases on the kernel from the SDK host context
AND it does not construct a separate kernel in the handler

scenarios.requirement: graph IPC methods use the Electron graph runtime
scenarios.name: Local graph IPC uses the Electron graph provider package
when: the desktop main process serves graph status, search, impact, hotspot, or index IPC requests
then: AND graph execution stays inside the Electron local host runtime

scenarios.requirement: graph IPC methods use the Electron graph runtime
scenarios.name: Renderer graph calls stay on the shared data port
when: the renderer requests graph data in desktop-local mode
then: it invokes the shared SpecdDataPort methods through IPC
AND the renderer does not import graph runtime packages directly
